description: Offline-first operation: all document CRUD, search, and graph traversal must work without network connectivity
CRDT-based collaborative editing: concurrent edits on the same document merge without user-facing conflicts
Persistent full-text index: InvertedIndex must survive process restart without full reindex from document store
LLM-powered summarization: replace LocalSummarizer stub with provider-backed implementation via Utilis
Plugin isolation enforcement: runtime validation that plugins cannot import outside PluginContext
Version branching and merge: support non-linear version histories with three-way merge on convergence
Graph-driven navigation: traverse document link topology visually with cluster-aware layout
Sync message durability: outbound messages that exhaust retry budget must persist to disk for later delivery
Hub-and-spoke: DocumentStore is the highest fan-in node — storage, sync, graph, versioning, indexing, and plugins all depend on it
Event-driven decoupling: StorageEventBus mediates all mutation side-effects; consumers subscribe via wildcard or typed events
Plugin sandbox boundary: PluginHost exposes PluginContext (read-only) to isolate third-party code from direct store access
Dual graph topology: spatial edges (DocumentLink via reference/related/derived_from/blocks) and temporal edges (VersionHistory parentVersionId chains) coexist
Vector clock causality: SyncEngine uses per-peer monotonic clocks with entrywise-max merge for conflict detection
Sync transport abstraction: SyncManager accepts any async send function — WebSocket, WebRTC, or direct call — no protocol lock-in
Immutable version snapshots: VersionHistory freezes every snapshot with Object.freeze; lineage chains are append-only
Separation of editing from storage: editor/operations.ts wraps store methods with ID generation but adds no business logic
SearchPlugin bypasses PluginContext and imports DocumentStore directly — violates the plugin sandbox contract
PresenceTracker imports both DocumentStore and SyncEngine, creating a compound boundary violation across two subsystems
LocalSummarizer is a sentence-extraction heuristic stub with no LLM integration — the Summarizer interface is unfulfilled
InvertedIndex stores all posting lists in memory with no serialization — every cold start requires full reindex
SyncQueue drops messages permanently after 5 retry attempts with no dead-letter persistence
VersionHistory diffs are line-based string comparison — no awareness of CRDT operations or semantic merge
DocumentStore.delete cascades link removal but does not cascade to VersionHistory — orphaned version chains persist
GraphBuilder.findClusters uses BFS connected components but has no feedback path to SyncManager for prioritizing cluster-local sync
StorageEventBus has no backpressure mechanism — a burst of mutations can flood subscribers synchronously
SyncEngine generates outbound messages for every storage event but applies no batching or coalescing for rapid edit sequences
Every document mutation (create, update, delete, link) must emit a StorageEvent before returning
Plugins must access documents exclusively through PluginContext — no direct store imports
Vector clocks must be monotonically non-decreasing per peer across all sync operations
Version snapshots are immutable after creation — no retroactive edits to historical versions
Document IDs must be globally unique across all peers in a sync group
Conflict resolution must produce an auditable ConflictRecord for every merge decision
Storage serialization format must remain JSON-compatible for local-first portability
Sync messages must be idempotent — replaying a message produces the same state as applying it once
SearchPlugin direct store access will cause stale search results when documents are modified through sync (bypasses event pipeline)
PresenceTracker importing SyncEngine creates a hidden circular dependency path: App→Sync→Store←Presence→Sync
Without index persistence, startup latency grows linearly with document count — unacceptable at 10k+ documents
SyncQueue message drops after 5 retries will cause permanent data divergence between peers in unreliable networks
Deleting a document without cascading to VersionHistory will cause getLineage to return orphaned chains with dangling references
Concurrent rapid edits without SyncEngine batching will generate O(n) sync messages where O(1) coalesced message suffices
The StorageEventBus wildcard subscriber pattern will become a bottleneck when plugin count exceeds 10 active subscribers
Line-based version diffs will produce false conflicts when two peers edit non-overlapping sections of the same document
Plugin sandbox claims isolation but SearchPlugin proves it is unenforced — architectural intent contradicts implementation
Local-first goal requires offline persistence but InvertedIndex and SyncQueue are memory-only with no disk fallback
SyncEngine has conflict resolution strategies but VersionHistory has no merge capability — sync and versioning are disconnected
StorageEventBus enables decoupling but PresenceTracker bypasses it entirely by importing subsystems directly
Summarizer interface declares an AI capability boundary but the only implementation is a non-AI heuristic stub
GraphBuilder detects document clusters but no component consumes cluster data for sync prioritization or UI grouping
Version snapshots are immutable but document content is mutable — the versioning layer has no trigger to auto-snapshot on edit
SyncManager supports pluggable transport but no concrete transport adapter exists — the abstraction is untested
